content.problemStatement:
For a fixed **binary** matrix BBB of size M√óNM \times NM√óN, hErd calls a **binary** array AAA of size NNN lucky if:


‚ąĎj=1NB(i,j)Aj‚Č°0(mod2)\displaystyle{\sum_{j=1}^{N} B_{(i,j)} A_{j} \equiv 0 \pmod{2}}j=1‚ąĎN‚ÄčB(i,j)‚ÄčAj‚Äč‚Č°0(mod2)  for all 1‚Č§i‚Č§M1 \leq i \leq M1‚Č§i‚Č§M
The matrix BBB is constructed by setting B(i,j) (1‚Č§i‚Č§M,1‚Č§j‚Č§N)B_{(i,j)} \ (1 \leq i \leq M,  1 \leq j \leq N)B(i,j)‚Äč (1‚Č§i‚Č§M,1‚Č§j‚Č§N) as 111 with probability p (0<p‚Č§1)p \ (0 \lt p \leq 1)p (0<p‚Č§1). Note that input for ppp is given in format XY\frac{X}{Y}YX‚Äč where X‚Č§YX \leq YX‚Č§Y.

For a positive integer sss, let EsE_{s}Es‚Äč be the expected number of lucky arrays AAA such that ‚ąĎi=1NAi=s\sum_{i=1}^{N} A_{i}=s‚ąĎi=1N‚ÄčAi‚Äč=s.

Given integer KKK, find the value ‚ąĎs=1KEs\sum_{s=1}^{K} E_{s}‚ąĎs=1K‚ÄčEs‚Äč.

It can be shown that the answer can be represented as PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP‚Äč, where PPP and QQQ are coprime integers and Q‚Č°Őł0(mod109+7)Q \not\equiv 0 \pmod{10^9+7}QÓÄ†‚Č°0(mod109+7). Print the value of P‚čÖQ‚ąí1(mod109+7)P \cdot Q^{-1} \pmod{10^9+7}P‚čÖQ‚ąí1(mod109+7).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
First and only line of each test contains 555 space-separated integers N,M,K,X,YN, M, K, X, YN,M,K,X,Y, as mentioned in the statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the value ‚ąĎs=1KEs\sum_{s=1}^{K} E_{s}‚ąĎs=1K‚ÄčEs‚Äč.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
The sum of MMM over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
The sum of KKK over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The array AAA has size 111, and matrix BBB has size 1√ó11\times 11√ó1.
Probability of B(i,j)=1B_{(i,j)} = 1B(i,j)‚Äč=1 is 12\frac{1}{2}21‚Äč. Therefore, the expected number of lucky arrays for s=1s = 1s=1 is 12=500000004mod‚ÄČ‚ÄČ(109+7)\frac{1}{2} = 500000004 \mod (10^9 + 7)21‚Äč=500000004mod(109+7).
For  s=2s = 2s=2, the expected number of lucky arrays is 000 because the sum can never be 222.
Hence, the final answer is 500000004500000004500000004.
